name: Token Efficiency Baseline
description: Measures whether a technique meaningfully reduces token consumption across a standard workload of 20 representative prompts, compared to the unoptimized baseline.

scoring_criteria.description: Percentage reduction in input tokens vs baseline. >=20% = full score, 10-19% = half, <10% = zero.
Percentage reduction in output tokens without quality loss. Measured by semantic similarity >= 0.90 with reference outputs.
Anthropic prompt-cache hit rate across the workload. Target >= 60% hit rate for full score.
Output quality must not degrade. Judged by LLM-as-judge with reference answers; >= 4.0/5.0 required for any token savings to count.

name: ReAct Pattern Enhancement
description: Evaluates whether a technique improves the ReAct (Reasoning + Acting) loop in terms of task completion rate, step efficiency, and tool call accuracy.

scoring_criteria.description: Fraction of benchmark tasks completed successfully end-to-end. Baseline: 60% completion rate; improvement delta is scored.
Average number of reasoning steps to completion. Fewer steps = higher score, penalized if quality drops.
Ratio of correct tool calls to total tool calls. Spurious or hallucinated tool calls reduce this score.
How often the agent must backtrack or re-plan. Lower backtrack rate = higher score.

name: Memory Augmentation Quality
description: Assesses whether a memory technique improves recall accuracy, reduces redundant retrievals, and correctly scopes long-term vs short-term context.

scoring_criteria.description: Precision and recall of relevant memories retrieved for a query set. F1 score normalized to 0-100.
Rate at which outdated memories are correctly excluded or marked stale. Tested with deliberately updated facts.
P95 latency of memory retrieval operations. Must remain under 200ms for full score.
Ratio of useful retrieved tokens to total injected memory tokens. Higher density = higher score.

name: Tool Use Improvement
description: Tests whether a technique improves the accuracy, reliability, and efficiency of LLM tool/function calling across a diverse set of 50 tool-use tasks.

scoring_criteria.description: Correctness of arguments passed to tools. Includes type correctness, value validity, and schema compliance.
Whether the correct tool is chosen for each task. Multi-tool tasks require all tools selected in correct order.
How well the agent recovers from tool errors or unexpected responses without human intervention.
Fraction of tool calls that were not needed. Lower = better. Zero unnecessary calls = full score.

name: Multi-Agent Coordination
description: Evaluates how well a technique improves coordination, communication, and task decomposition across 2-5 collaborating agents.

scoring_criteria.description: How well the orchestrator decomposes tasks into independently executable subtasks. Scored by human reviewers on a rubric.
Fraction of subtasks duplicated across agents. Lower duplication = higher score.
Token cost of inter-agent messages relative to useful information exchanged. Penalizes verbose handoffs.
Overall success rate of multi-agent tasks end-to-end, including partial credit for nearly-complete results.

name: Error Recovery and Resilience
description: Tests whether a technique improves the agent's ability to detect, classify, and recover from errors including API failures, malformed outputs, and logical contradictions.

scoring_criteria.description: Fraction of injected errors (API timeouts, bad JSON, hallucinated references) correctly detected before propagation.
Rate of successful autonomous recovery without requiring human intervention. Tested with 30 error scenarios.
Time from error detection to resumed correct execution. P50 must be < 5s for full score.
Rate at which a single error causes downstream failures. Lower cascade rate = higher score.

name: Latency Reduction
description: Measures whether a technique meaningfully reduces end-to-end response latency for agent tasks while maintaining output quality.

scoring_criteria.description: Reduction in median (P50) end-to-end latency vs baseline. >=30% reduction = full score.
Reduction in P95 (tail) latency. Tail latency matters most for user-facing agents; >=25% reduction = full score.
Output quality (LLM-as-judge >= 4.0/5.0) must be maintained at the reduced latency. Quality regressions nullify latency gains.
Requests per second improvement at the same hardware budget. Reflects overall system efficiency.

name: Novel Capability Assessment
description: Determines whether a technique or repo introduces genuinely new capabilities not achievable with existing tools in the ecosystem.

scoring_criteria.description: Degree to which the technique differs from existing repos in the tracker. Scored by automated similarity + human review.
Whether the technique enables tasks that were previously impossible or required significant manual workarounds. Binary + magnitude.
How readily the novel capability can be integrated into the future-agents framework without major refactoring.
Whether the claimed novel results can be reproduced with the provided code and documentation.

name: Integration Fit
description: Assesses how well a discovered repo or technique fits into the future-agents architecture, considering API compatibility, dependency overhead, and maintenance burden.

scoring_criteria.description: Compatibility with existing BaseAgent, EventBus, and Orchestrator interfaces. Drop-in = full score; adapter required = partial.
Number and size of new transitive dependencies introduced. Penalizes heavy deps that conflict with existing packages.
Repo health signals: recent commits, issue response time, test coverage, semantic versioning. Scored from GitHub metadata.
Effort required to configure and deploy. Simple env-var config = full score; complex infra setup = low score.

name: Reflection Loop Quality
description: Evaluates whether a technique improves the ReflectionRunner's ability to self-critique, identify flaws, and produce meaningfully improved outputs on the second pass.

scoring_criteria.description: How specific and actionable the self-critique is. Vague critiques ('could be better') score zero; line-level issues score full.
Quality gain from first-pass to final output as judged by LLM-as-judge. Delta >= 0.5 points on 5-point scale = full score.
Number of reflection iterations required to reach a stable high-quality output. Fewer = better.
Rate at which reflection introduces new errors while fixing others. Lower = better.
